creator: brooks@FreeBSD.org
text:
Thanks for your submission.  Sorry for taking to long to respond to it.

I do not intend to commit this patch to ca_root_nss for several reasons.

 - My intent with this port is to track security/nss.  The goal is that
   this certificate list be exactly the one in that port so that users
   have the most consistent view we can provide.  Another port could
   provide the tip of the tree if desired, but I won't do it.  For this
   reason I won't accept the CACert addition.

 - I've found no evidence that the ca-bundle.pl script is licensed for
   distribution except with modssl.  I've been too lazy to contact the
   contributor, but if someone did and got approval (or better yet a BSD
   license) we could do that.  I hate the modssl download, but avoiding
   it involves the risk of violating copyright.

 - Similarly, I haven't found a way to download a particular version of
   certdata.txt.  Since it's got a license we could just extract it and
   host it ourselves.  I should probably do that.

 - Using the unmodified ca-bundle.pl script means no verification is
   performed on the certdata.txt so someone who subverted the Mozilla
   anoncvs server could inject their own root certs.

I think adding the symlink is a good idea, but it's not generally
allowed in the ports framework.  I'm not sure what the right thing to do
there is.  Probably adding it as an OPTION is the right thing to do.

-- Brooks
